qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stored SQL injection via unsanitized column expression in dataset API
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes missing SQL sanitization on column expressions passed to literal_column() in the Dataset API. The advisory describes bypassing the read-only verification check on PostgreSQL connections in SQLLab. While both involve SQL in Superset, the root causes differ: this is a missing sanitize_clause() call on stored column expressions, whereas the advisory is about the read-only connection check failing to detect DML in crafted SQL statements. Different vulnerable function, different code path, different root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stored SQL injection via unsanitized fetch_values_predicate in dataset API
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes missing SQL sanitization on fetch_values_predicate passed to sqlalchemy.sql.text() in the Dataset API. The advisory describes bypassing the read-only verification check on PostgreSQL connections in SQLLab. Different root cause: this is unsanitized stored predicates bypassing sanitize_clause(), while the advisory is about the read-only connection verification failing to detect DML in specially crafted SQL. Different vulnerable function (get_fetch_values_predicate vs read-only check), different code path.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via database test connection endpoint with no private IP filtering
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF via the test_connection endpoint due to missing private IP filtering. The advisory describes a read-only bypass on PostgreSQL connections in SQLLab. Completely different vulnerability class (SSRF vs incorrect authorization/read-only bypass), different endpoint (test_connection vs SQLLab query execution), and different root cause (missing network restrictions vs insufficient DML detection in read-only checks).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-mwf2-qr4v-94h2) describes a read-only bypass on PostgreSQL connections in Apache Superset SQLLab, where the read-only verification check fails to detect DML in specially crafted SQL statements (CWE-863). None of the three qualifying findings identify this vulnerable code or root cause. The findings describe: (1) SQL injection via unsanitized column expressions in the Dataset API, (2) SQL injection via unsanitized fetch_values_predicate in the Dataset API, and (3) SSRF via the test_connection endpoint. Each targets a different vulnerable function, code path, and root cause than the advisory-described read-only verification bypass.
evidence: Advisory root cause: read-only verification check on PostgreSQL connections in SQLLab fails to detect DML in specially crafted SQL (CWE-863 Incorrect Authorization)

sqli-stored-column-expression: missing sanitize_clause() on literal_column() at models.py:908 - different root cause (SQL injection via stored column expressions vs read-only bypass on PostgreSQL connections)

sqli-stored-fetch-values-predicate: missing sanitize_clause() on self.text() at models.py:1387 - different root cause (SQL injection via stored predicates vs read-only bypass on PostgreSQL connections)
ssrf-database-test-connection: missing private IP filtering in test_connection at databases/api.py:1237 - different vulnerability class and root cause
